mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the unemployment rate signal a deteriorating labor market, reduced consumer spending, and lower corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns as the economy enters a recession.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has risen from 4.8% in Feb-08 to 5.7% in Jul-08
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during NBER-dated recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts indicates deep weakness in a key economic sector, reducing aggregate demand and negatively impacting financial sector stability and future corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have collapsed from a peak over 2,000k in 2006 to near 1,000k.
failure_condition: The 12-month change in HOUST shows no significant negative correlation (IC < -0.02) with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high and industrial production is falling, the central bank faces a stagflationary trade-off. This policy conflict creates uncertainty and risk aversion, leading to negative equity returns as investors anticipate either a policy error or prolonged economic weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2008 CPI was +5.6% YoY, while July INDPRO was -1.7% YoY.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly lower than periods where it is not met (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A negative trend in industrial production directly reflects lower output from factories, mines, and utilities. This decline in real economic activity translates into lower revenue and earnings for S&P 500 companies, causing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been in a downtrend since late 2007.
failure_condition: The 6-month momentum of INDPRO fails to show a positive rank correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Fed rate cuts are only bullish for equities when inflation is under control. When inflation is high, rate cuts are perceived as 'behind the curve' and insufficient to stimulate growth, providing no support for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has cut rates from 5.25% to 2.00%, but inflation remains elevated.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if rate cuts during high inflation periods (CPIAUCSL YoY > 4%) also produce positive forward returns, invalidating the regime condition.

mechanism_narrative: The monthly change in nonfarm payrolls is a high-frequency, comprehensive measure of economic health. Negative payroll prints signal contracting economic activity, leading investors to immediately revise earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown negative monthly changes for all of 2008.
failure_condition: The sign of the 1-month change in PAYEMS does not correctly predict the sign of the 5-day forward S&P 500 return more than 50% of the time over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth turns negative, it confirms a recession is underway. This confirmation removes ambiguity for investors, leading to broad-based selling of equities as future earnings estimates are slashed.
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2007 GDPC1 growth was negative, signaling the start of a downturn.
failure_condition: In the 60 days following a negative real GDP print, the S&P 500 posts a positive return in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and falling retail sales indicates severe stress on the consumer. This dual signal of weakening income and spending power reliably predicts lower corporate revenues and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment is up 1.1pp YoY, and retail sales growth has been weak.
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a 10-year backtest.
